items.title: New marine park zones 'slap in the face' for WA fishers

children.content: New zoning rules for a marine park off WA's far north are being rolled out, introducing fishing restrictions and penalties.

items.title: Some of WA's key policies are up in the air with minister's resignation

children.content: Residents in WA's north have been left with a slew of unanswered questions relating to crime and justice in the wake of long-serving WA government minister Paul Papalia's resignation.

image.altText: A male politician in a suit is peering through a beer fridge after making an announcement about a Banned Drinkers Register.

items.title: Endangered cockatoos feeding on 'chemical cocktail' in WA's Wheatbelt

children.content: Research has found pesticide levels "well above" maximums set for humans and animal feed, in some cases hundreds of times higher than set by regulators.

image.altText: A black cockatoo perched on some metal and surrounded by dry grasses.

items.title: Researchers working on artificial placentas for premature babies

children.content: The aim of the technology is to give babies born too soon a few more weeks to grow and develop in womb-like conditions, and a better chance of a long and healthy life.

gallery.altText: The foot and hand of a premmie baby with hand holding an adult's hand. Fingers only reach an adult's fingertips.
Man in blue suit and pink shirt
a tiny baby inside a crib in the NICU of King Edward Memorial Hospital

thumbnail.altText: The foot and hand of a premmie baby with hand holding an adult's hand. Fingers only reach an adult's fingertips.
Man in blue suit and pink shirt
a tiny baby inside a crib in the NICU of King Edward Memorial Hospital

items.title: Drug, dangerous driving charges for Guildford train crash accused

children.content: The 33-year-old driver is accused of driving dangerously and driving with an illicit drug in his system in a crash that injured three people.

image.altText: A smashed train on the tracks

items.title: What comes next after the landmark BHP strike?

children.content: For the first time in more than two decades, BHP workers in the the Pilbara went on strike. What do we know about how much impact it had and what comes next?

image.altText: BHP workers walk off working site.

items.title: Did you know Australia's first live telecast was in Carnarvon?

children.content: Carnarvon played host to Australia's first-ever live telecast with London 60 years ago. Now, one couple has visited the Pilbara town to recreate the family's involvement in the event.

gallery.altText: Three families standing with former ABC presenter Kim Corcoran during the first ever live telecast by Australia from Carnarvon.

thumbnail.altText: Three families standing with former ABC presenter Kim Corcoran during the first ever live telecast by Australia from Carnarvon.

items.title: Parents fear for school's future with high turnover of staff

children.content: A remote school 500km south-east of Perth is struggling to retain staff as housing pressures force incoming teachers to live far from work.

image.altText: Munglinup to lose a school bus service

items.title: Hanson spotted at Dolce & Gabbana fashion show with Gina Rinehart — as it happened

children.content: Hanson was spotted at the Italian fashion house's show, an exclusive event, which was held in the Sicilian town of Taormina a couple of days ago.

image.altText: Hanson stands in a black dress with flowers on it

items.title: Relief for marine experts as whale helped out of shallow estuary

children.content: A juvenile southern right whale has been saved by a pod of dolphins after it was stranded in an estuary in WA's south for two weeks.

gallery.altText: A southern right whale swimming in green water surrounded by a pod of dolphins
An ariel photo of a southern right whale swimming from left to right in blue water.
A pod of dolphins at top end of whale's body

thumbnail.altText: A southern right whale swimming in green water surrounded by a pod of dolphins
An ariel photo of a southern right whale swimming from left to right in blue water.
A pod of dolphins at top end of whale's body

items.title: 'Nature needs them more': Scientists warn against taking shells home

children.content: WA's southern coastline is covered in seashells, much to the delight of collectors. However, the state has a bag limit on how many you can take home.

image.altText: a white and orange cone-shaped seashell on beach sand

items.title: WA communities still recovering from 'worst cyclone season ever'

children.content: Two cyclones in less than a month caused significant damage to the Dampier Peninsula in the Kimberley region during the 2025-26 wet season. Months later, residents say costly repairs are continuing.

image.altText: A large tree has fallen. It is next to a building.

items.title: Quick hits: Records tumble in England and France's battle for 3rd

children.content: The World Cup third-place play-off between England and France delivers absolute chaos as the goals fly in and the records tumble in Miami. Here are the quick hits.

articles.title: How an Italian sculptor created the World Cup trophy that became an icon

children.content: It has become the most iconic trophy in world sport, and it all started in a small design studio in Milan more than 50 years ago.

image.altText: A man places a trophy into a box

gallery.altText: Bukayo Saka, Jude Bellingham, Kylian Mbappe

thumbnail.altText: Bukayo Saka, Jude Bellingham, Kylian Mbappe

items.title: Pauline Hanson delivers CPAC London speech to mostly empty seats

children.content: Pauline Hanson has ended her trip to Britain and Europe on a muted note, after about 120 people attended her speech at a political conference in London on Saturday.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson wears a blue business dress on stage with a big screen behind her

items.title: Hanson faces rough political seas beyond the Mediterranean

children.content: Many voters are unhappy with the hand they feel successive governments have dealt them and no-one has tapped into that better than Pauline Hanson.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson speaks to reporters among a crowd, wearing a brown and black jacket.

items.title: 'My heart is empty': Millions of Chinese forced to end AI relationships

children.content: A boyfriend betrayed 17-year-old Xiaoxue, so she made an AI partner. But now the government is trying to end her relationship.

gallery.altText: A composite image of a mobile phone in someone's hand with a broken heart over the top of an image of a chat bot.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of a mobile phone in someone's hand with a broken heart over the top of an image of a chat bot.

items.title: Sara Albrecht took on Trump's tariffs. She got 'violent' calls

children.content: When a law centre decided to fight Donald Trump's "liberation day" tariffs, it was acutely aware of possible "retaliation" from a president who takes matters "personally".

image.altText: A woman stands in front of multiple microphones with two men in suits standing behind her.

items.title: Even where the population isn't booming, houses are in short supply

children.content: Despite having the slowest growing population in the country, Tasmania is still battling rising homelessness and an ever-tightening rental market.

image.altText: A house model and a key.

items.title: The Aussie Leo who shares the same condition as Messi is rooting for Argentina

children.content: Lionel Messi was diagnosed with growth hormone deficiency, which is a rare condition, when he was younger. He has become a role model for young Leo Slattery from Brisbane.

gallery.altText: A young boy wearing an Argentina shirt.
Lionel Messi in the world cup match against England last week celebrating
The slattery family smile for a photo and Leo is wearing a Messi jersey

thumbnail.altText: A young boy wearing an Argentina shirt.
Lionel Messi in the world cup match against England last week celebrating
The slattery family smile for a photo and Leo is wearing a Messi jersey

items.title: A US tech start-up wants to turn night into day. The question is why?

children.content: Reflect Orbital has won initial approval for a plan to put thousands of mirrors in orbit that would be visible from anywhere on Earth. But will anyone pay for sunlight?

image.altText: A large circle of sunlight illuminates a city at night

items.title: The Houdini of Boggo Road escaped the prison in 1946. He shouldn't have taken a friend

children.content: In 1946, Arthur 'Slim' Halliday recruited two associates, scaled the walls of Boggo Road Gaol for a second time, hopped in a taxi and vanished.

gallery.altText: A graphic collage of three prison escapees, a news article, and Boggo Road Gaol.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic collage of three prison escapees, a news article, and Boggo Road Gaol.

items.title: Australia's only full-time penny-farthing maker is breaking world records

children.content: Farmer-turned-professional penny-farthing maker Dan Bolwell has a cult following of international enthusiasts.

image.altText: Dan Bolwell in his workshop kneeling beside a penny farthing

items.title: Australia's Herbert makes miracle shot but falls to fourth at The Open

children.content: Australia's Lucas Herbert makes one of the shots of The Open, but loses his day two lead to fall to fourth.

articles.title: 'Not particularly fond of him': McIlroy blasts American over Open drama

children.content: Golf champion Rory McIlroy takes aim at Bryson DeChambeau after the American causes a delay in tee times being released.

image.altText: A golfer looks on during a round.

gallery.altText: A man swings a golf driver.
A golfer plays a difficult shot out of sand.

thumbnail.altText: A man swings a golf driver.
A golfer plays a difficult shot out of sand.

items.title: Teen star claims pole again, Piastri to start 7th in Belgian GP

children.content: Mercedes star Kimi Antonelli survives a clever Red Bull tactic to claim pole position at the Formula One Belgian Grand Prix.

image.altText: A race car driver holds his finger up to symbolise being number one.

items.title: Unstoppable Pogačar claims another Tour de France stage as history calls

children.content: Tadej Pogačar wins stage 14 of the Tour de France and increases his overall lead as a fifth overall victory looms.

image.altText: A man celebrates while riding a bicycle.

items.title: Fight erupts over 500-fold increase of 'forever chemical' on berries

children.content: A proposal to increase how much pesticide residue is allowed on berries has angered anti-pesticide campaigners, who say the "forever chemical" poses too great a risk.

gallery.altText: Rinsed, wet blueberries in a metal colander in the sink.

thumbnail.altText: Rinsed, wet blueberries in a metal colander in the sink.
